```{r}
# Load required libraries
#install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2)
#install.packages("ggrepel")
library(ggrepel)
#install.packages("dplyr")
library(dplyr)
#install.packages("maps")
library(maps)
#install.packages("mapdata")
library(mapdata)
#install.packages("sf")
#library(sf)

############################### before run this file u should run three individual data import files first (forecast,obs,wow)

#Visualise all offical stations, cities and wow stations

# Create the combined map
unique_stations <- get_unique_wow_stations("2022-09-01")

# Create the combined map
gridpoint_station_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add Netherlands boundary using maps package
  borders("world", regions = "Netherlands", 
          fill = "white", colour = "black", size = 0.8) +
  
  # Connection lines between gridpoints and stations
  geom_segment(data = gridpoint_station_table,
               aes(x = gp_lon, y = gp_lat, xend = sta_lon, yend = sta_lat),
               alpha = 0.3) +
  
  # Plot gridpoint points (dark blue squares)
  geom_point(data = gridpoint_station_table,
             aes(x = gp_lon, y = gp_lat, shape = "Gridpoints"),
             color = "darkblue", size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Plot primary stations (red circles)
  geom_point(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
               filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "primary"),
             aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, shape = "Primary Stations"),
             color = "red", size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Plot secondary stations (yellow triangles)
  geom_point(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
               filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "secondary"),
             aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, shape = "Secondary Stations"),
             color = "gold", size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Plot WOW stations (green diamonds) - FIXED THIS PART
  geom_point(data = unique_stations,
             aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, shape = "WOW Stations"),
             color = "darkgreen", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.3) +
  
  # Set shapes for all point types
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Gridpoints" = 15,           # Square
                               "Primary Stations" = 16,       # Circle
                               "Secondary Stations" = 17,     # Triangle
                               "WOW Stations" = 18,          # Diamond
                               "Cities" = 19),               # Solid circle
                     name = "Type") +
  
  # Set size scale for cities
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6), guide = "none") +
  
  # Add gridpoint labels
  geom_text_repel(data = gridpoint_station_table,
                  aes(x = gp_lon, y = gp_lat, label = gridpoint),
                  size = 2.5,
                  color = "darkblue",
                  box.padding = 0.3,
                  point.padding = 0.2,
                  max.overlaps = 20) +
  
  # Add primary station labels
  geom_text_repel(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
                    filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "primary"),
                  aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, 
                      label = paste0("S", sta_id)),
                  size = 2,
                  color = "red",
                  nudge_y = 0.02,
                  box.padding = 0.2,
                  point.padding = 0.1,
                  max.overlaps = 20) +
  
  # Add secondary station labels
  geom_text_repel(data = gridpoint_station_table %>% 
                    filter(!is.na(sta_id) & station_type == "secondary"),
                  aes(x = sta_lon, y = sta_lat, 
                      label = paste0("S", sta_id)),
                  size = 2,
                  color = "gold",
                  nudge_y = -0.02,
                  box.padding = 0.2,
                  point.padding = 0.1,
                  max.overlaps = 20) +
  
  # Add city labels (for all cities)
  geom_text_repel(data = cities,
                  aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, label = city),
                  size = 2.5,
                  color = "purple",
                  fontface = "bold",
                  box.padding = 0.2,
                  point.padding = 0.2,
                  segment.size = 0.3,
                  segment.alpha = 0.5,
                  max.overlaps = 30,
                  force = 2) +
  
  # Titles and labels
  labs(title = "Weather Observation Network in the Netherlands",
       subtitle = "ECMWF Gridpoints, KNMI Stations (Primary/Secondary), WOW Stations, and Major Cities",
       x = "Longitude",
       y = "Latitude",
       caption = "Cities sized by population rank | WOW stations in green") +
  
  # Theme settings
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8, face = "italic")) +
  
  # Set coordinate limits to focus on Netherlands
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(3.3, 7.3), ylim = c(50.7, 53.6))

# Display the map
gridpoint_station_map

```

#find 1 official station and 5 WOW stations that cloese to big cities

```{r}
library(geosphere)

create_city_station_connections <- function(date_str, n_wow_stations = 5) {
  
  cat("Creating city-station connection table for", date_str, "...\n")
  
  # 1. Get WOW stations for this date
  unique_stations <- get_unique_wow_stations(date_str)
  # 2. Check if there are enough WOW stations
  if (nrow(unique_stations) < n_wow_stations) {
    warning(paste("Insufficient WOW stations! Only", nrow(unique_stations), "stations available"))
    n_wow_stations <- nrow(unique_stations)
  }
  
  # 3. Create base city_connections (if not already exists)
  # Assumes city_connections already exists with city and nearest official station info
  # If not, need to create it first
  
  # 4. Add WOW station information to city_connections
  city_connections_with_wow <- city_connections %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      # Calculate distances to all WOW stations
      wow_distances = list(
        distHaversine(
          cbind(city_lon, city_lat), 
          cbind(unique_stations$Longitude, unique_stations$Latitude)
        ) / 1000  # Convert to kilometers
      ),
      # Find indices of nearest n stations
      nearest_wow_idx = list(order(wow_distances)[1:min(n_wow_stations, length(wow_distances))]),
      # Get distances of nearest stations
      nearest_wow_distances = list(wow_distances[nearest_wow_idx]),
      # Get coordinates of nearest stations
      nearest_wow_lat = list(unique_stations$Latitude[nearest_wow_idx]),
      nearest_wow_lon = list(unique_stations$Longitude[nearest_wow_idx]),
      # Add date marker
      wow_data_date = date_str
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-wow_distances)  # Remove complete distance list
  
  # 5. Add useful summary information
  city_connections_with_wow <- city_connections_with_wow %>%
    mutate(
      # Distance to nearest WOW station
      closest_wow_distance = sapply(nearest_wow_distances, function(x) x[1]),
      # Whether WOW station is closer than official station
      wow_closer_than_official = closest_wow_distance < distance_km,
      # Distance improvement
      distance_improvement = distance_km - closest_wow_distance
    )
  
  # 6. Print summary statistics
  cat("\n===== Summary Statistics =====\n")
  cat("Date:", date_str, "\n")
  cat("Number of cities:", nrow(city_connections_with_wow), "\n")
  cat("Total WOW stations:", nrow(unique_stations), "\n")
  cat("WOW stations connected per city:", n_wow_stations, "\n")
  wow_better <- sum(city_connections_with_wow$wow_closer_than_official)
  cat("\nCities where WOW is closer than official:", wow_better, "/", nrow(city_connections_with_wow), "\n")
  cat("Average distance improvement:", 
      round(mean(city_connections_with_wow$distance_improvement[city_connections_with_wow$wow_closer_than_official]), 2), 
      "km\n")
  
  return(city_connections_with_wow)
}

#usage
city_connections_summer <- create_city_station_connections("2022-09-01")
city_connections_summer
```

#plot cities and its 5 closest WOW stations and 1 official station

```{r}
# Function to create city-station connection map
plot_city_station_connections <- function(city_connections_data, selected_cities = NULL) {
  
  # If specific cities are selected, filter the data
  if (!is.null(selected_cities)) {
    city_connections_data <- city_connections_data %>%
      filter(city %in% selected_cities)
  }
  
  # Prepare WOW stations data for plotting
  wow_stations_plot <- city_connections_data %>%
    select(city, nearest_wow_lat, nearest_wow_lon, nearest_wow_distances) %>%
    unnest(c(nearest_wow_lat, nearest_wow_lon, nearest_wow_distances)) %>%
    group_by(city) %>%
    mutate(
      wow_rank = row_number(),
      station_label = paste0("W", wow_rank)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Create the map
  city_station_map <- ggplot() +
    # Add Netherlands boundary
    borders("world", regions = "Netherlands", 
            fill = "white", colour = "black", size = 0.8) +
    
    # Connection lines from cities to official stations
    geom_segment(data = city_connections_data,
                 aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, 
                     xend = station_lon, yend = station_lat),
                 color = "red", alpha = 0.3, linetype = "solid", size = 0.8) +
    
    # Connection lines from cities to WOW stations
    geom_segment(data = wow_stations_plot,
                 aes(x = city_connections_data$city_lon[match(city, city_connections_data$city)], 
                     y = city_connections_data$city_lat[match(city, city_connections_data$city)], 
                     xend = nearest_wow_lon, yend = nearest_wow_lat),
                 color = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.2, linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5) +
    
    # Plot official stations (red circles)
    geom_point(data = city_connections_data,
               aes(x = station_lon, y = station_lat, shape = "Official Stations"),
               color = "red", size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    
    # Plot WOW stations (green diamonds)
    geom_point(data = wow_stations_plot,
               aes(x = nearest_wow_lon, y = nearest_wow_lat, shape = "WOW Stations"),
               color = "darkgreen", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.6) +
    
    # Plot cities (purple circles, sized by population)
    geom_point(data = city_connections_data,
               aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, shape = "Cities"),
               color = "purple", alpha = 0.8,size = 3.5) +
    
    # Set shapes
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("Official Stations" = 16,    # Circle
                                 "WOW Stations" = 18,          # Diamond
                                 "Cities" = 19),               # Solid circle
                       name = "Type") +
    
    # Set size scale for cities
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 7), guide = "none") +
    
    # Add city labels
    geom_text_repel(data = city_connections_data,
                    aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat, label = city),
                    size = 3,
                    color = "purple",
                    fontface = "bold",
                    box.padding = 0.3,
                    point.padding = 0.3,
                    max.overlaps = 30) +
    
    # Add official station labels
    geom_text_repel(data = city_connections_data,
                    aes(x = station_lon, y = station_lat, 
                        label = paste0("S", nearest_idx)),
                    size = 2,
                    color = "red",
                    box.padding = 0.2,
                    point.padding = 0.1,
                    max.overlaps = 20) +
    
    # Titles and labels
    labs(title = paste("City-Station Connections -", unique(city_connections_data$wow_data_date)),
         subtitle = "Cities with nearest Official Station (red) and 5 closest WOW Stations (green)",
         x = "Longitude",
         y = "Latitude",
         caption = "Solid lines: City to Official Station | Dashed lines: City to WOW Stations") +
    
    # Theme settings
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.box = "horizontal",
          legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
          plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8, face = "italic")) +
    
    # Set coordinate limits to focus on Netherlands
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(3.3, 7.3), ylim = c(50.7, 53.6))
  
  return(city_station_map)
}


#plot all
map_all <- plot_city_station_connections(city_connections_summer)
print(map_all)
```

#calcualte humudity and wet bulb temprature

```{r}
# Calculate vapor pressure
e <- function(T) {
  6.112 * exp((17.67 * T) / (T + 243.5))
}

# Calculate relative humidity
get_rh <- function(temp_C, dew_C) {
  100 * e(dew_C) / e(temp_C)
}

# Calculate Wet-Bulb Globe Temperature (WBGT)
# Based on empirical formulas for outdoor conditions with solar radiation
# Calculate Wet-Bulb Globe Temperature (WBGT) - vectorized version
get_wbgt <- function(temp_C, RH) {
  # Ensure RH is within reasonable bounds
  RH <- pmax(pmin(RH, 100), 1)
  
  # Calculate wet-bulb temperature using Stull equation
  T <- temp_C
  T_w <- T * atan(0.151977 * sqrt(RH + 8.313659)) +
         atan(T + RH) -
         atan(RH - 1.676331) +
         0.00391838 * RH^(3/2) * atan(0.023101 * RH) -
         4.686035
  
  # Vectorized WBGT calculation for outdoor conditions
  # Globe temperature approximation (varies with temperature)
  T_g <- ifelse(temp_C < 25,
                temp_C + 2,  # Low temperature - minimal solar effect
                temp_C + 2 + (temp_C - 25) * 0.1)  # Higher temperature - increased solar effect
  
  # Standard WBGT outdoor formula
  WBGT <- 0.7 * T_w + 0.2 * T_g + 0.1 * temp_C
  
  return(WBGT)
}
```

#combine forecast with obs

```{r}
combine_forecast_obs <- function(date_str, 
                               gridpoint_id, 
                               lead_time_hours = NULL,
                               aggregate_obs = "hourly",
                               include_metadata = TRUE) {
  
  # Get forecast data (now includes city information)
  date_only <- as.character(as.Date(date_str))
  forecast_data <- tidy_forecast_with_station_grid_city_location(date_str, gridpoint_id)
  
  # Filter by lead_time_hours if specified
  if (!is.null(lead_time_hours)) {
    # Handle single value or vector of lead times
    if (length(lead_time_hours) == 1) {
      forecast_data <- forecast_data %>%
        filter(lead_time_hrs == lead_time_hours)
      
      if (nrow(forecast_data) == 0) {
        stop(paste("No data found for lead_time_hrs =", lead_time_hours))
      }
      
      if (include_metadata) {
        cat("Filtered to lead_time_hrs =", lead_time_hours, "\n")
      }
    } else {
      # Multiple lead times specified
      forecast_data <- forecast_data %>%
        filter(lead_time_hrs %in% lead_time_hours)
      
      if (nrow(forecast_data) == 0) {
        stop(paste("No data found for lead_time_hrs in", paste(lead_time_hours, collapse = ", ")))
      }
      
      if (include_metadata) {
        cat("Filtered to lead_time_hrs in [", paste(lead_time_hours, collapse = ", "), "]\n", sep = "")
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Extract station_id early to fail fast
  station_id <- unique(forecast_data$station_id)
  if(is.na(station_id) || length(station_id) == 0) {
    warning("No station_id found for this gridpoint")
    return(forecast_data)
  }
  
  # Prepare date range
  start_date <- as.Date(date_str)
  date_range <- start_date + 0:9
  
  # Batch import observations - use list instead of repeated rbind
  all_obs_data <- list()
  for (i in seq_along(date_range)) {
    current_date_str <- as.character(date_range[i])
    tryCatch({
      daily_obs <- import_knmi_data(current_date_str)
      if (!is.null(daily_obs)) {
        all_obs_data[[i]] <- daily_obs
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      # Silent fail for individual dates
    })
  }
  
  # Combine all observation data at once
  if (length(all_obs_data) > 0) {
    obs_data <- bind_rows(all_obs_data)
  } else {
    warning("No observation data found for any date")
    return(forecast_data)
  }
  
  # Filter for specific station more efficiently
  pattern <- paste0("^", station_id, "_")
  obs_filtered <- obs_data %>%
    filter(grepl(pattern, LOCATION, fixed = FALSE)) %>%
    mutate(DTG = as.POSIXct(DTG, tz = "UTC"))
  
  if(nrow(obs_filtered) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No observations found for station", station_id))
    return(forecast_data)
  }
  
  # Ensure Valid_datetime is POSIXct
  forecast_data$Valid_datetime <- as.POSIXct(forecast_data$Valid_datetime, tz = "UTC")
  
  # Join and calculate derived variables in one pipeline
  combined_data <- forecast_data %>%
    left_join(obs_filtered, by = c("Valid_datetime" = "DTG"), suffix = c("_forecast", "_obs")) %>%
    mutate(
      # Calculate relative humidity and WBGT for forecast data
      Relative_humidity_pct = get_rh(Temperature_C, DewPoint_C),
      WBGT_C = get_wbgt(Temperature_C, Relative_humidity_pct),
      
      # Calculate relative humidity and WBGT for observed data at 10m
      RH_10 = get_rh(T_DRYB_10, T_DEWP_10),
      WBGT_obs_10 = get_wbgt(T_DRYB_10, RH_10)
    ) %>%
    # Relocate columns for better organization
    relocate(WBGT_C, .before = Gridpoint_ID) %>%
    relocate(Relative_humidity_pct, .before = Gridpoint_ID) %>%
    relocate(RH_10, .before = U_10) %>%
    relocate(WBGT_obs_10, .before = U_10)
  
  return(combined_data)
}

# Test examples

# Get all lead times (default behavior)
combined <- combine_forecast_obs("2021-01-31 12:00:00", 605)
unique(combined$lead_time_hrs)

# # Get only lead_time_hrs = 6
combined_6h <- combine_forecast_obs("2021-01-31 12:00:00", 605, lead_time_hours = 6)
unique(combined_6h$lead_time_hrs)  # Should only show 6
# 
# # Get multiple specific lead times
# combined_multi <- combine_forecast_obs("2021-01-31 12:00:00", 605, lead_time_hours = c(6, 12, 24))
# unique(combined_multi$lead_time_hrs)  # Should show 6, 12, 24
# 
# # Get a range of lead times
# combined_range <- combine_forecast_obs("2021-01-31 12:00:00", 605, lead_time_hours = seq(0, 48, by = 6))
# unique(combined_range$lead_time_hrs)  # Should show 0, 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, 36, 42, 48
```

############################## combine all data (WOW,official,forecast) #######################################

```{r}
combine_forecast_obs_wow <- function(date_str, 
                                    gridpoint_id, 
                                    lead_time_hours = NULL,
                                    aggregate_obs = "hourly",
                                    include_metadata = TRUE,
                                    n_wow_stations = 5,
                                    quality_filter = TRUE,
                                    min_quality = "Medium",
                                    include_quality_metrics = TRUE) {
  
  # Convert single lead_time to vector if needed
  if (!is.null(lead_time_hours) && !is.vector(lead_time_hours)) {
    lead_time_hours <- c(lead_time_hours)
  }
  
  # If lead_time_hours is NULL, use all available lead times
  if (is.null(lead_time_hours)) {
    # Define all possible lead times - every 6 hours from 0 to 240
    lead_time_hours <- seq(0, 240, by = 6)  # 0, 6, 12, 18, 24, ..., 240 (41 lead times total)
    message(paste("No lead times specified. Using all available lead times (0-240 hours, every 6 hours):",
                  "Total", length(lead_time_hours), "lead times."))
  }
  
  # OPTIMIZATION: Pre-calculate all unique dates needed across all lead times
  start_date <- as.Date(date_str)
  all_dates_needed <- c()
  
  for (lead_time in lead_time_hours) {
    days_ahead <- ceiling(lead_time / 24)
    
    if (lead_time <= 24) {
      date_range <- start_date + 0:1
    } else if (lead_time <= 48) {
      date_range <- start_date + 0:2
    } else if (lead_time <= 72) {
      date_range <- start_date + 0:3
    } else if (lead_time <= 120) {
      date_range <- start_date + 0:5
    } else if (lead_time <= 240) {
      date_range <- start_date + 0:10
    } else {
      date_range <- start_date + 0:min(14, days_ahead)
    }
    
    all_dates_needed <- c(all_dates_needed, as.character(date_range))
  }
  
  # Get unique dates to avoid duplicate WOW API calls
  unique_dates <- unique(all_dates_needed)
  message(paste("Will fetch WOW data for", length(unique_dates), "unique dates"))
  
  # STEP 1: Get forecast data for all lead times first (to get city coordinates)
  first_forecast <- combine_forecast_obs(
    date_str = date_str,
    gridpoint_id = gridpoint_id,
    lead_time_hours = lead_time_hours[1],
    aggregate_obs = aggregate_obs,
    include_metadata = FALSE
  )
  
  if (is.null(first_forecast) || nrow(first_forecast) == 0) {
    warning("No forecast data available. Exiting.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Extract city coordinates (same for all lead times)
  nearest_city_lat <- unique(first_forecast$nearest_city_lat)[1]
  nearest_city_lon <- unique(first_forecast$nearest_city_lon)[1]
  nearest_city_name <- unique(first_forecast$nearest_city)[1]
  
  if(is.na(nearest_city_lat) || is.na(nearest_city_lon)) {
    nearest_city_lat <- unique(first_forecast$gridpoint_lat)[1]
    nearest_city_lon <- unique(first_forecast$gridpoint_lon)[1]
  }
  
  # STEP 2: Fetch ALL WOW data once for all unique dates
  message("Fetching WOW data for all required dates...")
  all_wow_data <- list()
  
  for (date_to_fetch in unique_dates) {
    tryCatch({
      wow_obs <- import_WOW_nearest_stations(
        date_to_fetch, 
        nearest_city_lat,
        nearest_city_lon,
        n_stations = n_wow_stations,
        quality_filter = quality_filter,
        min_quality = min_quality
      )
      
      if (!is.null(wow_obs) && nrow(wow_obs) > 0) {
        all_wow_data[[date_to_fetch]] <- wow_obs
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      # Silent fail for individual dates
    })
  }
  
  message(paste("Successfully fetched WOW data for", length(all_wow_data), "dates"))
  
  # STEP 3: Process WOW data once if we have any
  wow_processed <- NULL
  
  if (length(all_wow_data) > 0) {
    wow_combined <- dplyr::bind_rows(all_wow_data)
    
    # Station ID should be standardized
    station_id_col <- "ExternalSiteId"
    
    if (!station_id_col %in% names(wow_combined)) {
      warning("ExternalSiteId not found, creating from coordinates")
      wow_combined <- wow_combined %>%
        mutate(
          lat_rounded = round(as.numeric(Latitude), 6),
          lon_rounded = round(as.numeric(Longitude), 6),
          ExternalSiteId = paste0("WOW_", lat_rounded, "_", lon_rounded)
        ) %>%
        select(-lat_rounded, -lon_rounded)
    }
    
    # Ensure all required variables are numeric
    wow_combined <- wow_combined %>%
      mutate(
        Latitude = as.numeric(Latitude),
        Longitude = as.numeric(Longitude),
        DryBulbTemperature_Celsius = as.numeric(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius),
        RelativeHumidity = as.numeric(RelativeHumidity),
        DewPointTemperature_Celsius = as.numeric(DewPointTemperature_Celsius),
        AirPressure_Hectopascal = if("AirPressure_Hectopascal" %in% names(.)) {
          as.numeric(AirPressure_Hectopascal)
        } else {
          NA_real_
        },
        MeanSeaLevelPressure_Hectopascal = if("MeanSeaLevelPressure_Hectopascal" %in% names(.)) {
          as.numeric(MeanSeaLevelPressure_Hectopascal)
        } else {
          NA_real_
        },
        WindSpeed_MetrePerSecond = if("WindSpeed_MetrePerSecond" %in% names(.)) {
          as.numeric(WindSpeed_MetrePerSecond)
        } else {
          NA_real_
        },
        WindDirection = if("WindDirection" %in% names(.)) {
          as.numeric(WindDirection)
        } else {
          NA_real_
        },
        RainfallAmount_Millimetre = if("RainfallAmount_Millimetre" %in% names(.)) {
          as.numeric(RainfallAmount_Millimetre)
        } else {
          NA_real_
        }
      )
    
    # Calculate WBGT with valid datetime
    wow_with_wbgt <- wow_combined %>%
      mutate(
        Valid_datetime = floor_date(ReportEndDateTime, "hour"),
        wow_wbgt = ifelse(
          !is.na(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius) & !is.na(RelativeHumidity),
          get_wbgt(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, RelativeHumidity),
          NA_real_
        )
      )
    
    # Create individual station summaries with quality metrics
    station_summaries <- wow_with_wbgt %>%
      group_by(Valid_datetime, ExternalSiteId, Latitude, Longitude) %>%
      summarise(
        station_temp = mean(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_humidity = mean(RelativeHumidity, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_dewpoint = mean(DewPointTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_pressure = mean(AirPressure_Hectopascal, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_mslp = mean(MeanSeaLevelPressure_Hectopascal, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_wind_speed = mean(WindSpeed_MetrePerSecond, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_wind_dir = mean(WindDirection, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_rainfall = sum(RainfallAmount_Millimetre, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_wbgt = mean(wow_wbgt, na.rm = TRUE),
        station_n_obs = n(),
        
        # Add quality metrics if available
        station_quality = if("quality_category" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          first(quality_category)
        } else NA_character_,
        station_confidence = if("confidence_score" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          first(confidence_score)
        } else NA_real_,
        station_pass_rate = if("pass_rate" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          first(pass_rate)
        } else NA_real_,
        station_qc_flags = if("qc_flag" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          paste(table(qc_flag), collapse = "/")
        } else NA_character_,
        
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      group_by(Valid_datetime) %>%
      arrange(desc(station_wbgt)) %>%
      mutate(station_rank = row_number()) %>%
      filter(station_rank <= n_wow_stations) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    # Create wide format for individual stations with quality info
    quality_cols <- if(include_quality_metrics) {
      c("station_quality", "station_confidence", "station_pass_rate", "station_qc_flags")
    } else {
      character(0)
    }
    
    station_wide <- station_summaries %>%
      pivot_wider(
        id_cols = Valid_datetime,
        names_from = station_rank,
        values_from = c(ExternalSiteId, Latitude, Longitude, 
                       station_temp, station_humidity, station_dewpoint,
                       station_pressure, station_mslp, 
                       station_wind_speed, station_wind_dir,
                       station_rainfall, station_wbgt, station_n_obs,
                       all_of(quality_cols)),
        names_glue = "wow_station_{station_rank}_{.value}"
      )
    
    # Aggregated statistics with quality weighting
    wow_aggregated <- wow_with_wbgt %>%
      group_by(Valid_datetime) %>%
      summarise(
        # Temperature statistics
        wow_temp_mean = mean(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_temp_sd = sd(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_temp_min = min(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_temp_max = max(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_temp_q25 = quantile(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_temp_q75 = quantile(DryBulbTemperature_Celsius, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Humidity statistics
        wow_humidity_mean = mean(RelativeHumidity, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_humidity_sd = sd(RelativeHumidity, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_humidity_min = min(RelativeHumidity, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_humidity_max = max(RelativeHumidity, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Dewpoint statistics
        wow_dewpoint_mean = mean(DewPointTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_dewpoint_max = max(DewPointTemperature_Celsius, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Pressure statistics
        wow_pressure_mean = mean(AirPressure_Hectopascal, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_mslp_mean = mean(MeanSeaLevelPressure_Hectopascal, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Wind statistics
        wow_wind_speed_mean = mean(WindSpeed_MetrePerSecond, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_wind_speed_max = max(WindSpeed_MetrePerSecond, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Rainfall statistics
        wow_rainfall_sum = sum(RainfallAmount_Millimetre, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_rainfall_max = max(RainfallAmount_Millimetre, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # WBGT statistics
        wow_wbgt_mean = mean(wow_wbgt, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_wbgt_sd = sd(wow_wbgt, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_wbgt_min = min(wow_wbgt, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_wbgt_max = max(wow_wbgt, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_wbgt_q25 = quantile(wow_wbgt, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        wow_wbgt_q75 = quantile(wow_wbgt, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Station with max WBGT
        wow_wbgt_max_station_id = if(any(!is.na(wow_wbgt))) {
          ExternalSiteId[which.max(wow_wbgt)][1]
        } else NA_character_,
        
        # Quality statistics (if available)
        wow_n_high_quality = if("quality_category" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          sum(quality_category == "High", na.rm = TRUE)
        } else 0,
        wow_n_medium_quality = if("quality_category" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          sum(quality_category == "Medium", na.rm = TRUE)
        } else 0,
        wow_n_low_quality = if("quality_category" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          sum(quality_category == "Low", na.rm = TRUE)
        } else 0,
        wow_mean_confidence = if("confidence_score" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          mean(confidence_score, na.rm = TRUE)
        } else NA_real_,
        wow_mean_pass_rate = if("pass_rate" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          mean(pass_rate, na.rm = TRUE)
        } else NA_real_,
        
        # Metadata
        wow_n_stations = n_distinct(ExternalSiteId),
        wow_n_obs = n(),
        wow_n_qc_passed = if("qc_flag" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          sum(qc_flag == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
        } else NA_integer_,
        wow_n_qc_suspicious = if("qc_flag" %in% names(wow_with_wbgt)) {
          sum(qc_flag == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
        } else NA_integer_,
        
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        # Clean infinite values
        across(where(is.numeric), ~ifelse(is.infinite(.), NA, .)),
        
        # Derived metrics
        wow_temp_range = wow_temp_max - wow_temp_min,
        wow_humidity_range = wow_humidity_max - wow_humidity_min,
        wow_wbgt_range = wow_wbgt_max - wow_wbgt_min,
        wow_wbgt_iqr = wow_wbgt_q75 - wow_wbgt_q25,
        wow_wbgt_anomaly = wow_wbgt_max - wow_wbgt_mean,
        
        # Quality indicators
        wow_quality_ratio = ifelse(
          !is.na(wow_n_high_quality) & !is.na(wow_n_stations) & wow_n_stations > 0,
          (wow_n_high_quality + 0.5 * wow_n_medium_quality) / wow_n_stations,
          NA_real_
        ),
        wow_qc_pass_ratio = ifelse(
          !is.na(wow_n_qc_passed) & !is.na(wow_n_obs) & wow_n_obs > 0,
          wow_n_qc_passed / wow_n_obs,
          NA_real_
        ),
        
        # Search metadata
        wow_city_searched = nearest_city_name,
        wow_search_lat = nearest_city_lat,
        wow_search_lon = nearest_city_lon,
        wow_quality_filter_applied = quality_filter,
        wow_min_quality_required = min_quality
      )
    
    # Combine aggregated and individual station data
    wow_processed <- wow_aggregated %>%
      left_join(station_wide, by = "Valid_datetime")
  }
  
  # STEP 4: Now process each lead time and merge with WOW data
  message("Processing forecast data for each lead time...")
  all_lead_time_results <- list()
  
  for (lead_time in lead_time_hours) {
    
    # Get forecast + KNMI obs for this specific lead time
    combined_data <- combine_forecast_obs(
      date_str = date_str,
      gridpoint_id = gridpoint_id,
      lead_time_hours = lead_time,
      aggregate_obs = aggregate_obs,
      include_metadata = FALSE
    )
    
    # Skip if no data returned for this lead time
    if (is.null(combined_data) || nrow(combined_data) == 0) {
      message(paste("No forecast data available for lead time:", lead_time, "hours. Skipping..."))
      next
    }
    
    # Join with processed WOW data
    if (!is.null(wow_processed)) {
      combined_data <- combined_data %>%
        left_join(wow_processed, by = "Valid_datetime") %>%
        mutate(
          wow_data_available = !is.na(wow_n_stations),
          wow_coverage = ifelse(!is.na(wow_n_stations), wow_n_stations / n_wow_stations, 0),
          wow_data_quality = case_when(
            is.na(wow_mean_confidence) ~ "No Quality Data",
            wow_mean_confidence >= 0.8 ~ "Excellent",
            wow_mean_confidence >= 0.6 ~ "Good",
            wow_mean_confidence >= 0.4 ~ "Fair",
            wow_mean_confidence >= 0.2 ~ "Poor",
            TRUE ~ "Very Poor"
          ),
          lead_time = lead_time  # Add lead time column for tracking
        )
    } else {
      # No WOW data available
      combined_data <- combined_data %>%
        mutate(
          wow_data_available = FALSE,
          wow_coverage = 0,
          wow_data_quality = "No Data",
          wow_quality_filter_applied = quality_filter,
          wow_min_quality_required = min_quality,
          lead_time = lead_time
        )
    }
    
    # Store the results for this lead time
    all_lead_time_results[[as.character(lead_time)]] <- combined_data
  }
  
  # Combine results from all lead times
  if (length(all_lead_time_results) > 0) {
    final_result <- dplyr::bind_rows(all_lead_time_results)
    
    # Add metadata if requested
    if (include_metadata) {
      final_result <- final_result %>%
        mutate(
          n_lead_times_processed = length(lead_time_hours),
          lead_times_requested = paste(lead_time_hours, collapse = ", "),
          n_unique_wow_dates_fetched = length(unique_dates),
          processing_timestamp = Sys.time()
        )
    }
    
    message(paste("Processing complete. Total rows:", nrow(final_result)))
    return(final_result)
  } else {
    warning("No data retrieved for any of the specified lead times")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Test examples with different lead_time inputs
# Single lead time (original behavior)
system.time(result1 <- combine_forecast_obs_wow("2021-07-27 12:00:00", 1002, 6, n_wow_stations = 5))
result1

# Multiple specific lead times - note that 6 and 24 will share some WOW data
system.time(result2 <- combine_forecast_obs_wow("2021-07-27 12:00:00", 891, c(6, 12, 24, 48), n_wow_stations = 5))
result2

# Every 12 hours for first 3 days
#result3 <- combine_forecast_obs_wow("2021-07-27 12:00:00", 891, seq(0, 72, by = 12), n_wow_stations = 5)

# All lead times (NULL) - will use 0, 6, 12, 18, ..., 240 hours (41 lead times)
# WOW data will be fetched only once for all unique dates
#result4 <- combine_forecast_obs_wow("2021-07-27 12:00:00", 891, NULL, n_wow_stations = 5)

```

#data combination visualisation !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

```{r}
plot_forecast_obs_wow_spaghetti <- function(forecast_datetime, 
                                            gridpoint_id, 
                                            variable = "Temperature_C", 
                                            members = 1:51,
                                            n_wow_stations = 5,
                                            wow_statistic = "mean",  # "mean" or "max"
                                            show_mean = FALSE) {
  
  # Validate wow_statistic parameter
  if (!wow_statistic %in% c("mean", "max")) {
    stop("wow_statistic must be either 'mean' or 'max'")
  }
  
  # Variable mapping - updated for WBGT
  var_mapping <- list(
    "Temperature_C" = list(
      forecast = "Temperature_C",
      obs = "T_DRYB_10",
      wow_mean = "wow_temp_mean",
      wow_max = "wow_temp_max",
      label = "Temperature (°C)"
    ),
    "Humidity_pct" = list(
      forecast = "Relative_humidity_pct",  
      obs = "RH_10",
      wow_mean = "wow_humidity_mean",
      wow_max = "wow_humidity_max",
      label = "Relative Humidity (%)"
    ),
    "WBGT_C" = list(  # Changed from WetBulb_C to WBGT_C
      forecast = "WBGT_C",
      obs = "WBGT_obs_10",  # Changed from WBGT_cal_10 to match your function
      wow_mean = "wow_wbgt_mean",  # Changed from wow_WBGT_mean
      wow_max = "wow_wbgt_max",    # Changed from wow_WBGT_max
      label = "Wet Bulb Globe Temperature (°C)"
    )
  )
  
  if (!variable %in% names(var_mapping)) {
    stop(paste("Invalid variable:", variable, 
               ". Valid options are:", paste(names(var_mapping), collapse = ", ")))
  }
  
  var_info <- var_mapping[[variable]]
  forecast_var_name <- var_info$forecast
  obs_var_name <- var_info$obs
  wow_var_name <- if(wow_statistic == "mean") var_info$wow_mean else var_info$wow_max
  y_label <- var_info$label
  
  # Get the combined forecast, official obs, and WOW data
  cat("Fetching combined data...\n")
  combined <- combine_forecast_obs_wow(
    date_str = forecast_datetime, 
    gridpoint_id = gridpoint_id,
    n_wow_stations = n_wow_stations
  )
  
  if (is.null(combined) || nrow(combined) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No combined data found for", forecast_datetime, "at gridpoint", gridpoint_id))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Check if forecast variable exists
  if (!forecast_var_name %in% names(combined)) {
    cat("Available forecast columns:", paste(names(combined), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    stop(paste("Forecast variable", forecast_var_name, "not found in data"))
  }
  
  # Check if WOW variable exists
  if (!wow_var_name %in% names(combined)) {
    cat("Available WOW columns:", paste(grep("^wow_", names(combined), value = TRUE), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    warning(paste("WOW variable", wow_var_name, "not found in data"))
  }
  
  # Filter for selected members
  member_names <- paste0("M", members)
  plot_data <- combined %>%
    filter(Member %in% member_names)
  
  # Get observation data (take first value per lead time)
  obs_data <- plot_data %>%
    group_by(lead_time_hrs) %>%
    summarise(
      obs_value = first(.data[[obs_var_name]]),
      wow_value = first(.data[[wow_var_name]]),
      wow_n_stations = first(wow_n_stations),
      # Also get range statistics if using max for WBGT
      wow_range = if(wow_statistic == "max" && variable == "WBGT_C") {
        first(wow_wbgt_range)  # Changed from wow_wetbulb_range
      } else NA,
      wow_anomaly = if(wow_statistic == "max" && variable == "WBGT_C") {
        first(wow_wbgt_anomaly)  # Changed from wow_wetbulb_anomaly
      } else NA,
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Create spaghetti plot
  p <- ggplot() +
    # Spaghetti lines for all ensemble members
    geom_line(data = plot_data,
              aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = .data[[forecast_var_name]], group = Member),
              alpha = 0.3, 
              color = "gray50", 
              size = 0.4)
  
  # Add ensemble mean if requested
  if (show_mean) {
    p <- p +
      stat_summary(data = plot_data,
                   aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = .data[[forecast_var_name]]),
                   fun = mean, 
                   geom = "line", 
                   color = "black", 
                   size = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.9)
  }
  
  # Add official observations (blue)
  p <- p +
    geom_line(data = obs_data %>% filter(!is.na(obs_value)),
              aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = obs_value),
              color = "blue", 
              size = 1.2) +
    geom_point(data = obs_data %>% filter(!is.na(obs_value)),
               aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = obs_value),
               color = "darkblue", 
               size = 2, 
               alpha = 0.8)
  
  # Add WOW crowd-sourced observations
  wow_color <- if(wow_statistic == "max") "red" else "orange"
  wow_point_color <- if(wow_statistic == "max") "darkred" else "darkorange"
  
  p <- p +
    geom_line(data = obs_data %>% filter(!is.na(wow_value)),
              aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = wow_value),
              color = wow_color, 
              size = 1.2) +
    geom_point(data = obs_data %>% filter(!is.na(wow_value)),
               aes(x = lead_time_hrs, y = wow_value),
               color = wow_point_color, 
               size = 2, 
               alpha = 0.8)
  
  # Optional: Add shaded area showing WOW range when using max for WBGT
  if (wow_statistic == "max" && variable == "WBGT_C") {
    obs_data_ribbon <- obs_data %>%
      filter(!is.na(wow_value) & !is.na(wow_anomaly)) %>%
      mutate(
        wow_mean_calc = wow_value - wow_anomaly  # Calculate mean from max and anomaly
      )
    
    if (nrow(obs_data_ribbon) > 0) {
      p <- p +
        geom_ribbon(data = obs_data_ribbon,
                    aes(x = lead_time_hrs, 
                        ymin = wow_mean_calc, 
                        ymax = wow_value),
                    fill = "red", 
                    alpha = 0.1)
    }
  }
  
  # Get station info
  station_info <- unique(plot_data$station_id)[1]
  gridpoint_lat <- round(unique(plot_data$gridpoint_lat)[1], 2)
  gridpoint_lon <- round(unique(plot_data$gridpoint_lon)[1], 2)
  
  # Create descriptive caption
  wow_description <- if(wow_statistic == "max") {
    paste("Red: WOW maximum (peak heat stress from", n_wow_stations, "nearest stations)")
  } else {
    paste("Orange: WOW mean (average from", n_wow_stations, "nearest stations)")
  }
  
  # Labels and theme
  p <- p +
    labs(
      title = paste("Forecast Spaghetti Plot -", 
                    format(as.POSIXct(forecast_datetime), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")),
      subtitle = paste("Gridpoint", gridpoint_id, "(", gridpoint_lat, "°N,", gridpoint_lon, "°E) |",
                      "Station:", station_info, "|",
                      length(members), "ensemble members |",
                      "WOW:", wow_statistic),
      x = "Lead Time (hours)",
      y = y_label,
      caption = paste("Gray: Ensemble members | Blue: Official obs |", 
                     wow_description,
                     if(show_mean) "| Black: Ensemble mean" else "")
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray40"),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray50", hjust = 0),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 240, 24)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 240))
  
  # Add day separation lines
  p <- p +
    geom_vline(xintercept = seq(24, 240, 24), alpha = 0.2, linetype = "dashed")
  
  return(p)
}

# Usage example:
# plot_forecast_obs_wow_spaghetti("2022-09-01 12:00:00", 818, variable = "WBGT_C", wow_statistic = "max")

# Usage examples:
# For mean WOW values (default):
plot_forecast_obs_wow_spaghetti("2021-09-01 12:00:00", 891, variable = "WBGT_C", wow_statistic = "mean")

# For maximum WOW values (captures peak heat stress):
plot_forecast_obs_wow_spaghetti("2021-09-01 12:00:00", 891, variable = "WBGT_C", wow_statistic = "max")
```
# temp and wbgt three sources comparison
```{r}
# Fixed version of the distance relationship plot
plot_heat_distance_relationship <- function(combined_data) {
  
  # Prepare data - extract station data
  distance_data <- combined_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(wow_wbgt_max)) %>%
    select(Valid_datetime, 
           starts_with("wow_station_"),
           T_DRYB_10, WBGT_obs_10) %>%
    slice_sample(n = min(100, nrow(.)))  # Sample to avoid too many points
  
  # Reshape data - create empty dataframe first
  station_data <- data.frame()
  
  for(i in 1:5) {
    dist_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_avg_distance_km")
    temp_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_temp")
    wbgt_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_wbgt")
    
    if(all(c(dist_col, temp_col, wbgt_col) %in% names(distance_data))) {
      temp_df <- distance_data %>%
        select(Valid_datetime, all_of(c(dist_col, temp_col, wbgt_col))) %>%
        rename(
          distance = !!dist_col,
          temperature = !!temp_col,
          wbgt = !!wbgt_col
        ) %>%
        mutate(station = paste0("WOW_", i)) %>%
        filter(!is.na(distance))
      
      if(nrow(temp_df) > 0) {
        station_data <- rbind(station_data, temp_df)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Check if we have data
  if(nrow(station_data) == 0) {
    cat("No station distance data available\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create plots
  library(patchwork)
  
  p1 <- ggplot(station_data, aes(x = distance, y = temperature)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = station), alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "Temperature vs Distance from City",
         x = "Distance from City Center (km)",
         y = "Temperature (°C)") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  p2 <- ggplot(station_data, aes(x = distance, y = wbgt)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = station), alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "WBGT vs Distance from City",
         x = "Distance from City Center (km)",
         y = "WBGT (°C)") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  return(p1 / p2)
}

# Alternative simpler visualization if the above doesn't work
plot_heat_comparison <- function(combined_data) {
  
  # Simple comparison plot
  comparison_data <- combined_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(wow_temp_mean)) %>%
    select(Valid_datetime, 
           KNMI_Temp = T_DRYB_10,
           WOW_Mean = wow_temp_mean,
           WOW_Max = wow_temp_max,
           KNMI_WBGT = WBGT_obs_10,
           WOW_WBGT_Mean = wow_wbgt_mean,
           WOW_WBGT_Max = wow_wbgt_max)
  
  # Temperature comparison
  p1 <- comparison_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(KNMI_Temp, WOW_Mean, WOW_Max),
                 names_to = "Source",
                 values_to = "Temperature") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Valid_datetime, y = Temperature, color = Source)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("KNMI_Temp" = "blue", 
                                  "WOW_Mean" = "orange", 
                                  "WOW_Max" = "red")) +
    labs(title = "Temperature Comparison: KNMI vs WOW",
         y = "Temperature (°C)",
         x = "Time") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # WBGT comparison
  p2 <- comparison_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(KNMI_WBGT, WOW_WBGT_Mean, WOW_WBGT_Max),
                 names_to = "Source",
                 values_to = "WBGT") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Valid_datetime, y = WBGT, color = Source)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("KNMI_WBGT" = "blue", 
                                  "WOW_WBGT_Mean" = "orange", 
                                  "WOW_WBGT_Max" = "red")) +
    labs(title = "WBGT Comparison: KNMI vs WOW",
         y = "WBGT (°C)",
         x = "Time") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  library(patchwork)
  return(p1 / p2)
}

#test
result <- combine_forecast_obs_wow("2021-09-01 12:00:00", 1002,6, n_wow_stations = 5)
p_comparison <- plot_heat_comparison(result)
print(p_comparison)
```
#location relationship
```{r}
plot_station_map <- function(combined_data, time_point = NULL) {
  
  library(ggplot2)
  library(sf)
  library(rnaturalearth)
  library(rnaturalearthdata)
  
  # 选择时间点
  if(is.null(time_point)) {
    plot_data <- combined_data %>%
      filter(!is.na(wow_wbgt_max)) %>%
      slice(1)
  } else {
    plot_data <- combined_data %>%
      filter(Valid_datetime == time_point)
  }
  
  # 获取荷兰地图
  netherlands <- ne_countries(scale = "large", country = "Netherlands", returnclass = "sf")
  
  # 准备站点数据
  stations_df <- data.frame()
  
  # 添加城市中心
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$nearest_city_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$nearest_city_lat[1],
    type = "City Center",
    name = plot_data$nearest_city[1],
    temperature = NA,
    wbgt = NA,
    distance_km = 0
  ))
  
  # 添加KNMI站点
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$station_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$station_lat[1],
    type = "KNMI Station",
    name = plot_data$station_id[1],
    temperature = plot_data$T_DRYB_10[1],
    wbgt = plot_data$WBGT_obs_10[1],
    distance_km = plot_data$nearest_city_distance_km[1]
  ))
  
  # 添加格点位置
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$gridpoint_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$gridpoint_lat[1],
    type = "Gridpoint",
    name = paste("Grid", plot_data$Gridpoint_ID[1]),
    temperature = plot_data$Temperature_C[1],
    wbgt = plot_data$WBGT_C[1],
    distance_km = NA
  ))
  
  # 计算地图范围
  lon_range <- range(c(plot_data$nearest_city_lon[1], 
                       plot_data$station_lon[1], 
                       plot_data$gridpoint_lon[1]), na.rm = TRUE)
  lat_range <- range(c(plot_data$nearest_city_lat[1], 
                       plot_data$station_lat[1], 
                       plot_data$gridpoint_lat[1]), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # 扩展范围
  lon_buffer <- diff(lon_range) * 0.5
  lat_buffer <- diff(lat_range) * 0.5
  
  # 创建基础地图
  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = netherlands, fill = "lightgray", color = "darkgray") +
    coord_sf(xlim = lon_range + c(-lon_buffer, lon_buffer),
             ylim = lat_range + c(-lat_buffer, lat_buffer)) +
    
    # 添加站点
    geom_point(data = stations_df,
               aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
                   color = wbgt, 
                   shape = type, 
                   size = type),
               alpha = 0.8) +
    
    # 添加标签
    geom_text(data = stations_df,
              aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = name),
              vjust = -1.5, size = 3) +
    
    # 添加距离圆圈（可选）
    geom_point(data = stations_df[stations_df$type == "City Center",],
               aes(x = lon, y = lat),
               shape = 21, size = 10, stroke = 2, 
               color = "red", fill = NA) +
    
    scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "yellow", high = "red",
                          midpoint = 20, 
                          name = "WBGT (°C)",
                          na.value = "gray50") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("City Center" = 15, 
                                  "KNMI Station" = 17, 
                                  "Gridpoint" = 16,
                                  "WOW Station" = 19)) +
    scale_size_manual(values = c("City Center" = 5, 
                                 "KNMI Station" = 4, 
                                 "Gridpoint" = 3,
                                 "WOW Station" = 3)) +
    
    labs(title = paste("Station Locations and Heat Stress Distribution"),
         subtitle = paste("Time:", plot_data$Valid_datetime[1], 
                         "| City:", plot_data$nearest_city[1]),
         shape = "Location Type",
         size = "Location Type") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
  
  return(p)
}

# 创建一个更详细的版本，包含WOW站点（如果有坐标信息）
plot_detailed_station_map <- function(combined_data, wow_station_coords = NULL) {
  
  library(ggplot2)
  library(geosphere)
  
  # 选择有WOW数据的时间点
  plot_data <- combined_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(wow_wbgt_max)) %>%
    slice(1)
  
  city_lon <- plot_data$nearest_city_lon[1]
  city_lat <- plot_data$nearest_city_lat[1]
  
  # 创建站点数据框
  stations <- data.frame(
    lon = c(city_lon, plot_data$station_lon[1], plot_data$gridpoint_lon[1]),
    lat = c(city_lat, plot_data$station_lat[1], plot_data$gridpoint_lat[1]),
    type = c("City", "KNMI", "Gridpoint"),
    value = c(NA, plot_data$WBGT_obs_10[1], plot_data$WBGT_C[1]),
    label = c(plot_data$nearest_city[1], 
              plot_data$station_id[1], 
              paste("Grid", plot_data$Gridpoint_ID[1]))
  )
  
  # 如果有WOW站点坐标，添加模拟的WOW站点
  if(is.null(wow_station_coords)) {
    # 模拟5个WOW站点在不同距离
    set.seed(123)
    for(i in 1:5) {
      angle <- runif(1, 0, 2*pi)
      distance <- i * 2 # km
      # 简单的距离转换（不精确但足够演示）
      delta_lon <- (distance / 111) * cos(angle)
      delta_lat <- (distance / 111) * sin(angle)
      
      temp_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_temp")
      wbgt_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_wbgt")
      
      if(wbgt_col %in% names(plot_data) && !is.na(plot_data[[wbgt_col]][1])) {
        stations <- rbind(stations, data.frame(
          lon = city_lon + delta_lon,
          lat = city_lat + delta_lat,
          type = paste0("WOW", i),
          value = plot_data[[wbgt_col]][1],
          label = paste0("WOW", i)
        ))
      }
    }
  }
  
  # 创建图
  p <- ggplot(stations) +
    # 添加距离圆圈
    geom_point(data = stations[stations$type == "City",],
               aes(x = lon, y = lat),
               shape = 21, size = 20, stroke = 1, 
               color = "gray70", fill = NA, alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_point(data = stations[stations$type == "City",],
               aes(x = lon, y = lat),
               shape = 21, size = 40, stroke = 1, 
               color = "gray70", fill = NA, alpha = 0.2) +
    geom_point(data = stations[stations$type == "City",],
               aes(x = lon, y = lat),
               shape = 21, size = 60, stroke = 1, 
               color = "gray70", fill = NA, alpha = 0.1) +
    
    # 添加连线
    geom_segment(data = stations[stations$type != "City",],
                 aes(x = city_lon, y = city_lat,
                     xend = lon, yend = lat),
                 color = "gray50", linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
    
    # 添加站点
    geom_point(aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
                   color = value, 
                   shape = type),
               size = 5) +
    
    # 添加标签
    geom_text(aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = label),
              vjust = -1.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    
    # 添加数值标签
    geom_text(data = stations[!is.na(stations$value),],
              aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
                  label = paste0(round(value, 1), "°C")),
              vjust = 1.5, size = 2.5) +
    
    scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "yellow", high = "red",
                          midpoint = 20, 
                          name = "WBGT (°C)",
                          na.value = "gray50") +
    
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1.3) +
    
    labs(title = "Spatial Distribution of Weather Stations",
         subtitle = paste("City:", plot_data$nearest_city[1], 
                         "| Time:", format(plot_data$Valid_datetime[1], "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")),
         x = "Longitude",
         y = "Latitude") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  return(p)
}


# 生成地图
map1 <- plot_station_map(result)
map2 <- plot_detailed_station_map(result)

print(map1)
print(map2)

```
